Carbon · Case Study

100% Carbon Neutral Cleaning: Proprietary Calculation Across Scope 1, 2 and 3

CPC's Carbon Neutral Cleaning Program delivers verified emissions tracking and 100% offset across the entire client portfolio, applicable to government, defence, industrial and commercial contracts.

Sustainability · Cross-sector · Applicable to all contracts

  1. Home
  2. /
  3. Case Studies
  4. /
  5. 100% Carbon Neutral Cleaning: Proprietary Calculation Across Scope 1, 2 and 3
100%
Carbon neutral across portfolio
Scope 1+2+3
Full emissions tracked
Site-level
Granular reporting capability
ISO 14001 + CN
Dual certification held

On This Page

Progress

0%

Share

On this page

The Brief

Carbon neutral claims in the cleaning industry are common. Defensible, auditable, scope-complete carbon neutral programs are not. The difference matters because clients making sustainability commitments — net zero targets, science-based targets, ESG procurement requirements, supply chain emission reporting — need their service providers' claims to withstand scrutiny in their own sustainability disclosures. An unaudited carbon neutral claim that excludes Scope 3 emissions, or that relies on a single annual portfolio offset rather than site-level calculation, creates a disclosure risk for the client, not just a marketing gap for the provider.

The cleaning industry's carbon footprint is primarily Scope 3 — supply chain inputs including cleaning products, equipment manufacturing, upstream transport, and waste disposal. A carbon neutral claim that covers only Scope 1 (fuel combustion) and Scope 2 (purchased electricity) while omitting Scope 3 measures the smaller portion of the actual emissions footprint. For clients whose sustainability reporting covers full supply chain emissions, a provider whose carbon neutral calculation is Scope 1 and 2 only is not delivering a carbon neutral service — they are delivering a partially measured service.

The Approach

CPC's Carbon Neutral Cleaning Program is built on proprietary measurement infrastructure — the CPC Carbon Neutral Calculator — combined with verified offset procurement and dual certification under Carbon Neutral certification and ISO 14001.

The CPC Carbon Neutral Calculator tracks emissions across all three scopes:

  • Scope 1 emissions. Direct fuel combustion from vehicle fleet travel between sites, calculated using verified emission factors and actual fuel consumption data.
  • Scope 2 emissions. Purchased electricity used in cleaning equipment and site infrastructure, calculated using location-based and market-based methods and Australian grid emission factors.
  • Scope 3 emissions. Supply chain inputs including cleaning products (from production to use to disposal), equipment manufacturing, upstream transport, business travel, and operational waste. These are measured using supplier data, Australian Government DCCEEW National Greenhouse Accounts emission factors, and product-specific lifecycle data where available.

The calculator produces site-level outputs — each facility in the portfolio receives its own emissions calculation, not a share of a single portfolio total. This is the critical architectural feature that enables client-specific reporting: a government agency managing 50 sites with CPC can report on the carbon footprint of their specific cleaning contract, not a blended industry average.

The Operation

The Carbon Neutral Cleaning Program is available across CPC's entire portfolio — government, defence, industrial, education and commercial contracts. There is no minimum contract size for access to the program; the calculation methodology applies uniformly to a single-site contract and a 200-site national panel.

Offsets are purchased annually on behalf of the entire CPC portfolio through verified carbon credit providers. Offset project types include Australian reforestation and land management projects (generating Australian Carbon Credit Units, or ACCUs), renewable energy projects and internationally recognised community development projects that meet the additionality and permanence requirements of the Carbon Neutral certification standard.

Client reporting is produced at the site level and can be formatted for integration into the client's own ESG or sustainability reporting. For clients with external sustainability reporting requirements — annual reports, TCFD disclosures, government sustainability reporting frameworks — the CPC Carbon Neutral Calculator output provides the documented supplier evidence that these frameworks require.

Compliance and Standards

The Carbon Neutral Cleaning Program operates under two certification frameworks:

  • Carbon Neutral certification. Annual certification that CPC's operations are 100% carbon neutral, with audit of the measurement methodology, emission factor sources, and offset procurement. Certification is renewed annually and is publicly listed on the certification register, enabling independent verification by procurement teams without requiring CPC to provide documentation on request.
  • ISO 14001:2015. Environmental Management System certification, providing the documented framework within which the carbon measurement program, emission reduction initiatives, chemical management and waste minimisation operate as a management system rather than a standalone reporting exercise.

The measurement methodology uses DCCEEW National Greenhouse Accounts emission factors — the Australian Government's authoritative emission factor source — for all calculated emission categories where Australian factors are available. International IPCC factors are used for emission categories without Australian-specific factors.

Outcomes

100% carbon neutral certification across the CPC portfolio, renewed annually, is the primary outcome. The certification is independently audited — not self-assessed — and publicly listed, enabling any procurement team to verify the certification status without requesting documentation.

Site-level reporting capability means that any CPC client can obtain a documented emissions calculation and offset allocation for their specific contract. For clients with externally assured sustainability reports, this is the evidence standard required — a site-specific, auditable calculation rather than an estimate.

The program's applicability across the full CPC portfolio means that clients do not need to negotiate carbon neutral service as a special condition or premium offering. It is the standard operating condition for every CPC contract, applicable to any facility type and any contract scale.

For procurement teams assessing supplier ESG credentials, CPC's Carbon Neutral Cleaning Program demonstrates the difference between a carbon neutral claim and a carbon neutral program — one is a marketing position, the other is a documented, certified, independently verified management system with client-accessible reporting.

What This Demonstrates

Key Takeaways

  • Most cleaning providers that claim 'carbon neutral' don't measure Scope 3 — the largest emission source for a service business. CPC's Carbon Neutral Calculator tracks all three scopes, including supply chain inputs.
  • The CPC Carbon Neutral Calculator produces auditable, site-level reporting, not a single annual portfolio estimate. Each client can receive their specific emissions footprint and offset allocation.
  • 100% offset via verified carbon credit providers means government and corporate ESG procurement requirements are met out-of-the-box — no additional offset purchasing required by the client.
  • The program is portable across any contract type — a national government panel, a single-site facility, or a multi-state industrial portfolio. The calculation methodology applies uniformly.
  • For procurement teams under net zero commitments or science-based targets, this is one of the few cleaning offerings that can be independently defended in an externally assured sustainability report.

Why This Matters for Procurement

For government, corporate and institutional procurement teams operating under net zero commitments, ESG reporting requirements or science-based targets, the question is not whether your cleaning provider claims to be carbon neutral — it is whether their methodology can be defended in your sustainability report. CPC's Carbon Neutral Calculator produces site-level reporting with auditable inputs and verified offsets that meet this standard. For any client whose sustainability reporting is externally assured, a cleaning provider whose carbon neutral claim rests on an unaudited annual estimate creates a disclosure risk that a properly documented program eliminates.
